description:
# Callsite outcomes — unify the three callsite walks

Three passes walk every callsite today (`ir_reducer`, `ir_inline`,
`walk_spec_for_discovery`), each makes a partial decision, none of
them tell the others what they did. The reducer fails silently; the
typer re-derives what the reducer already saw; `ir_inline` decides
structurally with no Descr knowledge; `boundary_fns` is consulted by
some passes and not others.

This epic makes the callsite outcome first-class data on the Module:

```
struct CallsiteId { caller: FnId, block: BlockId, slot: EmitSlot }
enum CallsiteOutcome {
    Consumed { result: Descr },   // reducer folded it
    Inlined,                       // body spliced (subsumes ir_inline)
    Emitted  { target: SpecKey },  // typer minted a spec
    Stalled,                       // provisional: nobody decided
}
Module.callsite_outcomes: HashMap<CallsiteId, CallsiteOutcome>
```

Three passes become readers and writers of one table. Same shape,
shared vocabulary. Removals follow.

See `docs/callsite-outcomes.md` for the full design, game-walk, and
findings. The DAG below is the order to land the work in.

## What dies

- `ir_inline::inline_module` and its three sub-passes (after Phase E).
- `walk_spec_for_discovery`'s direct/closure_lit/closure_known emit
  paths (after Phase F).
- The reducer's silent failure mode.
- The duplicated `boundary_fns` checks across passes.

## What survives

- `ir_reducer` (rebadged as the outcomes writer).
- `ir_typer` (now a reader of `Emitted` outcomes + spec body typer +
  effective_returns + SCC widening).
- `inline_single_use_conts` (typer-output-driven, unrelated).
- `Module.boundary_fns` and the new `Module.callsite_outcomes`.

## Supersedes

This epic supersedes the deferred / blocked tail of fz-jg5:

- fz-jg5.9  — RED.8a (the closure_lit branch wasn't dead; the new
  framing absorbs it as an outcome producer rather than removing it
  outright)
- fz-jg5.10 — RED.8b (depended on .9)
- fz-jg5.11 — RED.8c (delete ir_inline; achieved cleanly via
  Phase E here)

Those tickets get a comment pointing here, then are closed as
superseded.
title: [EPIC] Callsite outcomes — one model for fold/inline/emit/stall
